title: Finish packing shit in morning — YEBW departure pep-talk
html:
TL;DR

  • Aug 11, 2019 three-line departure note: "-finish packing shit in morning / -do it in YEBW / -let go motherfuxket" — written the night before leaving for YEBW week in Portland.
  • "Do it in YEBW" is the action-mantra for the program week — Yahir going in with a commit-fully posture.
  • "Let go motherfuxket" (sic, typo for motherfucker) — release-anxiety self-talk; same second person pep talk register as the airplane goodbye letter.
  • Bookends the Ondine Hall logistics note from a month earlier — together they bracket the arrival → execution arc.
  • Shortest entry in the YEBW arc but the densest emotional preparation — three lines, three layers (logistics / commit / release).

Key quotes

"do it in YEBW"

"let go motherfuxket"

Why it matters in the graph

Three lines that isolate the YEBW program as the first high-stakes solo arena Yahir explicitly preps himself for in writing. The commit + release pairing is a pattern that recurs in Austin move-prep, Shanghai departure notes, and Vietnam route prep.

The note is also a clean instance of the second person pep talk convention before the airplane-goodbye-to-Alexandria cements the form a month later. Yahir is rehearsing on YEBW the emotional-prep mode he'll deploy at every later inflection point.
